title: Pass down the default config for buildah run command
body:
Currently we are not passing down the default environent as specified
in the containers.conf file.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3536

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Not sure if we should remove these environment variables when container stops running?
Needs a rebase and all kinds of test unhappiness @rhatdan

> @stellarpower  PTAL

Please take a look? Was wondering if there's a glossary for all these acronyms, I quite like them! Thanks!
@rhatdan I am not entirely sure but I think this is voiding the comment added on line `107` of `run_linux.go` more context is added here https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2635

@stellarpower I can confirm docker does not sets  `TERM` either. If we bake `TERM` in images i think we will loose parity between buildah and docker builds. I tried following with docker

```Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/alpine
RUN env
```
I think this issue might be a potential **`WONTFIX`**

Ah okay, so that'll carry through to the image. I hadn't thought of it like that.

Is there a way environment variables could be set for the shell session but not added to the image? Am guessing they're treayed as if they were ENV statements in a container file then (?). Could they be added to the session (possibly then not doing this by default) without going into the image?  With the proviso that this could change the resulting image if the process inside behaves differently - we could assume it's safe to neglect this for things like TERM, but of course passing any old environment variable through will impact the build process on the inside.

Would something like a mask potentially work?
If the image doesn't already contain this variable, run the `buildah run` command with it set, but don't add it to the image environment.
If the image does contain this variable, shadow the default coming from the user's shell behind the one already in the image.

Thanks for looking into it so quickly!
We have secret environment variables, that do not get committed to the image.
Looks like this is a bad idea, closing.
